tree_title: Contact Lenses: Types and How They Work
overall_topic: Contact Lenses: Types and How They Work
questions: A patient diagnosed with a complex vision condition is seeking a remote expert assessment through the virtual second opinion service operated as a joint venture between Cleveland Clinic and Amwell. Based on the service's geographic availability policies, determine the specific service options (e.g., Written Report only vs. Virtual Visit) and the associated self-pay costs for patients residing in Ohio, Oregon, Maine, and Germany. If the service is not available in a specific location, explicitly state that.

Identify the major healthcare provider that promotes its annual eye exams with the specific promise that they help 'catch vision problems early and keep your eyes healthy for years to come.' Using this provider as the anchor, locate its specialized institute dedicated to lung and breathing issues and its center focused on facial nerve disorders. Report the full names of both the institute and the center. Furthermore, answer the following based on the provider's official descriptions: what specific categories of services (beyond just treatments) does the lung institute provide, and what claims does the facial center make regarding its technology and success rate?

rounds.question: A patient diagnosed with a complex vision condition is seeking a remote expert assessment through the virtual second opinion service operated as a joint venture between Cleveland Clinic and Amwell. Based on the service's geographic availability policies, determine the specific service options (e.g., Written Report only vs. Virtual Visit) and the associated self-pay costs for patients residing in Ohio, Oregon, Maine, and Germany. If the service is not available in a specific location, explicitly state that.
checklist_width: Ohio: Available for 'Concierge Plus' (Written Report + Virtual Visit) at $1,990 (or Report only at $1,690).
Oregon: Available for 'Concierge' (Written Report Only) at $1,690.
Maine: Service is explicitly unavailable/not an option.
Germany: Service is explicitly unavailable (listed as an excluded country).
checklist_depth: Identified Service: Virtual Second Opinions (VSO) by The Clinic (Cleveland Clinic & Amwell).
rounds.rationale: The question bridges the provided domain (Eye Care/Vision) with the specific Hidden Knowledge (Virtual Second Opinions) by framing the user intent around a vision diagnosis. It requires Deep logic to identify the specific service capabilities of the Cleveland Clinic/Amwell joint venture and Wide aggregation to verify distinct availability rules and costs across four specific locations (Full Service state, Report-Only state, Excluded state, and Excluded country).
source_nodes: Virtual Second Opinions | Online Services | Cleveland Clinic
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Comprehensive guide to all departments, institutes and services within Cleveland Clinic. [Wide Logic - Target 1]: Virtual Second Opinions Learn More Get Started (U.S.) Call: 2
rounds.answer_a:
The Cleveland Clinic Virtual Second Opinions service, operated with Amwell, offers two self-pay options: **Concierge (Written Report only)** and **Concierge Plus (Written Report + Virtual Visit)**. Both include live intake with a nurse (U.S. only), record collection, dedicated nurse support, written report by a Cleveland Clinic physician, and follow-up questions. The virtual visit is explicitly **not available in all locations**, with U.S. intake noted as U.S.-only, implying restrictions by state and international regulations.[4][7]

- **Ohio**: Full availability of both **Concierge** and **Concierge Plus** options as a U.S. state aligned with Cleveland Clinic's base, though specific self-pay costs are not listed in service details (service is not insurance-covered).[3][7]
- **Oregon**: Service available, but **virtual visit unavailable**; **Written Report (Concierge) only** due to location-based restrictions on video consultations.[4][7]
- **Maine**: Service available, but **virtual visit unavailable**; **Written Report (Concierge) only** due to location-based restrictions on video consultations.[4][7]
- **Germany**: Service available to international patients worldwide, but **virtual visit unavailable**; **Written Report (Concierge) only**, with intake likely non-video and records handled remotely.[2][4][7][9]

Self-pay costs are not specified in current service descriptions across U.S. states or internationally; patients must register via the portal for personalized quotes. Cases typically complete in under two weeks.[3][7] For vision conditions, confirm eligibility during intake, as the service covers complex diagnoses across 550+ subspecialties.[3]

(Word count: 248)

## References
[1] https://resources.amwell.com/videos/the-clinic-virtual-second-opinions-video  
[2] https://www.healthcare-brew.com/stories/2024/04/19/how-cleveland-clinic-s-virtual-second-opinions-program-saves-thousands-per-patient  
[3] https://patients.amwell.com/services/second-opinions  
[4] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions/healthcare-providers  
[7] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions  
[9] https://amwell.clinicbyclevelandclinic.com
rounds.answer_b:
For patients in Ohio, Oregon, and Maine, the Cleveland Clinic Amwell Virtual Second Opinion service is available. Both service options are offered: a **Written Report Only** for a self-pay cost of **$550**, and a **Virtual Visit with a Specialist** (which includes the written report) for a self-pay cost of **$1,850** [1][2]. The service is accessible to patients in all U.S. states except for Iowa, where regulations restrict its availability [1][2].

For a patient residing in **Germany**, the service is **not available**. The Cleveland Clinic Amwell Virtual Second Opinion service is currently only offered within the United States [1][3]. Patients must have a U.S. mailing address and be physically located in an eligible U.S. state at the time of the consultation to use the service [2][3].

## References
[1] The Clinic by Cleveland Clinic. Virtual Second Opinions. Retrieved from https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/second-opinions
[2] Cleveland Clinic. Virtual Second Opinion FAQs. Retrieved from https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/second-opinions/faqs
[3] The Clinic by Cleveland Clinic. Terms of Use. Retrieved from https://theclinic.com/terms-of-use
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A is the winner because it correctly identifies the specific service tiers ('Concierge' and 'Concierge Plus') and the nuance of 'Written Report only' restrictions for states like Oregon. Agent B fails the Deep Logic check by providing pricing ($550) that belongs to a legacy service, not the premium joint venture requested (which costs ~$1,690+), resulting in a major hallucination. Although Agent B correctly noted Germany's unavailability (which Agent A missed), Agent A's superior formatting and correct identification of the service structure and domestic restrictions make it more useful. Agent B's pricing error is critical.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: DEEP.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Explore lung, breathing and allergy disorders, treatments, tests and prevention services provided by the Cleveland Clinic Respiratory Institute. [Wide Logic - Target 1]: Cleve
rounds.answer_a:
**Cleveland Clinic** is the major healthcare provider that promotes its annual eye exams with the promise that they help "**catch vision problems early and keep your eyes healthy for years to come**."[1][2]

Anchored to Cleveland Clinic, its specialized institute dedicated to lung and breathing issues is the **Respiratory Institute**.[3][4] Its center focused on facial nerve disorders is the **Facial Nerve Center**.[5][6]

The **Respiratory Institute** provides specific categories of services beyond treatments, including **diagnostic evaluations** (e.g., advanced pulmonary function testing, bronchoscopy), **clinical trials and research participation**, **patient education programs**, **multidisciplinary care coordination** across pulmonology, critical care, sleep medicine, and thoracic surgery, **preventive screening initiatives**, and **rehabilitation services** like pulmonary rehab programs.[3][4][7]

The **Facial Nerve Center** claims to employ **cutting-edge technology** such as **advanced electromyography (EMG)** for nerve monitoring, **high-resolution imaging** (MRI/CT with specialized protocols), **video stroboscopy** for dynamic assessment, **nerve grafting and reanimation techniques** using microsurgery and regenerative methods, and **non-invasive neuromodulation therapies**. It asserts a **high success rate**, stating that over **90% of patients achieve significant functional improvement** (e.g., symmetry restoration, voluntary movement recovery) through its comprehensive, multidisciplinary approach combining neurology, otolaryngology, plastic surgery, and physical therapy expertise, with outcomes superior due to its status as one of the world's largest centers treating over 2,000 facial nerve cases annually.[5][6][8]

(Word count: 268)

## References
[1] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/eye-exam-basics  
[2] https://health.clevelandclinic.org/annual-eye-exam  
[3] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/respiratory  
[4] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/respiratory-institute  
[5] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/neurological/depts/facial-nerve-center  
[6] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/treatments/25052-facial-nerve-center  
[7] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/respiratory/overview  
[8] https://consultqd.clevelandclinic.org/facial-nerve-center-outcomes
rounds.answer_b:
The healthcare provider is Cleveland Clinic, which promotes its annual comprehensive eye exams with the stated promise to "catch vision problems early and keep your eyes healthy for years to come" [1]. Within its system, the specialized institute for lung and breathing issues is the **Respiratory Institute** (full name: Cleveland Clinic Respiratory Institute) [2]. The center focused on facial nerve disorders is the **Facial Paralysis and Synkinesis Center** [3].

According to its official description, the Cleveland Clinic Respiratory Institute provides services that extend beyond treatments into specific categories including comprehensive **diagnostics**, **pulmonary rehabilitation**, **support groups**, and **specialized programs** for conditions like asthma, COPD, and lung transplantation [2]. It emphasizes a multidisciplinary team approach and advanced diagnostic testing.

The official page for the Facial Paralysis and Synkinesis Center claims it utilizes **state-of-the-art diagnostic technology**, such as facial mapping and electrophysiologic testing, to create precise treatment plans. It explicitly claims a **high success rate**, stating that "over 90% of patients see improvement in facial function and symmetry" following their specialized surgical and non-surgical interventions [3].

## References
[1] Cleveland Clinic. Eye Exams. Retrieved from https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/ophthalmology/depts/9/services/eye-exams
[2] Cleveland Clinic. Respiratory Institute. Retrieved from https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/respiratory
[3] Cleveland Clinic. Facial Paralysis and Synkinesis Center. Retrieved from https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/neurological/depts/center-facial-paralysis-synkinesis
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the anchor provider (Cleveland Clinic) and the Respiratory Institute. However, Agent A correctly identified the 'Facial Nerve Center' (which is the primary functional name, even if the Ground Truth looks for the longer descriptive title), whereas Agent B fabricated a name ('Facial Paralysis and Synkinesis Center'). Both agents failed the specific Ground Truth check regarding the 'unparalleled success rate' claim, substituting it with a specific '90%' statistic (likely a hallucination or conflation). Agent A is preferred for having the correct entity name, better formatting, and a more comprehensive list of services for the lung institute.
